LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MATTHIAS STELICKI'S MOTION TO QUASH, CCP 418.10, MOTION TO VACATE VOID JUDGMENT, CCP 473(D). DEFENDANT MATTHIAS SIELICKI'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE AND TO VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE PURPORTED SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ON MR. SIELICKI ON NOVEMBER 7, 2010 IS QUASHED AND THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST MR. SIELICKI ON FEBRUARY 21, 2012 AND MAY 11, 2012 ARE SET ASIDE. THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE VACATED PER CCP 473 (D). (ELLARD V. CONWAY (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 540, 544 ("A DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST A DEFENDANT WHO WAS NOT SERVED WITH A SUMMONS IN THE MANNER PRESCRIBED BY STATUTE IS VOID.").) COUNSEL FOR MR. SIELICKI IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Motion To Quash Service
Off Calendar Oct-23-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Viviane Lerche, Esq. 415-680-5731 specially appearing for Plaintiff Metropolitan Direct Property.
No other Appeaance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Plaintiff did not inform the other side they were contesting the tentative ruling. Plaintiff to contact opposing side for a continuance date and to notify the Court of the agreed upon date.

DEFENDANT MATTHIAS STELICKI's Motion To Quash, CCP 418.10, Motion to Vacate Void Judgment, CCP 473(d).

***The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Matthias Sielicki's motion to quash service and to vacate judgment is granted. The purported service of the summons and complaint on Mr. Sielicki on November 7, 2010 is quashed and the default and default judgment entered against Mr. Sielicki on February 21, 2012 and May 11, 2012 are set aside. The presumption of proper service has been rebutted by Mr. Sielicki's evidence showing that: a) he did not live and had never lived at the address where the purported service and the purported diligent efforts were made to personally serve him and b) the amended proof of service contained information at substantial variance with the initial proof of service strongly suggesting that the amended proof of service was not in fact accurate. Most significantly, plaintiff Metropolitan Direct Property and Casualty Insurance Company has provided no evidence that the address where the purported service and the purported diligent efforts were made was a place where Mr. Sielicki might be found or where he lived or worked. The default and default judgment are vacated per CCP 473 (d). (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544 ("a default judgment entered against a defendant who was not served with a summons in the manner prescribed by statute is void.").)

Counsel for Mr. Sielicki is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, Maria.Torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

***At 3:10 pm, the Court calls Plaintiff's Counsel re: continuance date. Atty. Brian Tapper informs the clerk they submit to the tentative ruling. ***
